service_line_config.name: dotnet
service_line_config.tech_context: This project is built with .NET technologies. Key considerations: - Framework: .NET 8+ (or .NET Framework for legacy), ASP.NET Core - API: ASP.NET Core Web API, Minimal APIs, gRPC services - ORM: Entity Framework Core, Dapper for high-performance queries - Architecture patterns: Clean Architecture, CQRS, MediatR, Repository pattern - Authentication: ASP.NET Identity, OAuth 2.0, Azure AD / Entra ID, JWT - Messaging: MassTransit, RabbitMQ, Azure Service Bus - Caching: IDistributedCache, Redis, MemoryCache - Testing: xUnit, NUnit, Moq, FluentAssertions, integration test fixtures - DevOps: MSBuild, NuGet, Docker, GitHub Actions or Azure DevOps pipelines - Hosting: IIS, Kestrel, Azure App Service, Azure Container Apps, Azure Kubernetes Service

artifact_overrides.module_extraction: Map modules to .NET solution projects or bounded contexts. Each module should correspond to a logical service, library, or domain area in the solution structure.
artifact_overrides.functional_req: - Reference .NET patterns and libraries where applicable (EF Core, Identity, MediatR) - For API requirements, specify endpoint patterns (REST conventions, versioning) - For data requirements, reference EF Core entities, migrations, and query patterns - Note authentication and authorization approach for protected features
artifact_overrides.nonfunctional_req: - API response time and throughput benchmarks - EF Core query performance and N+1 prevention - Connection pool sizing and database connection management - Memory usage and garbage collection considerations for high-traffic endpoints - Logging and observability (Serilog, Application Insights, OpenTelemetry) - Health check and readiness probe requirements
artifact_overrides.task: - Solution structure setup: projects, layers, NuGet packages - Domain model: entities, value objects, EF Core configuration, migrations - API development: controllers/minimal APIs, request/response models, validation - Service layer: business logic, MediatR handlers, repository implementation - Authentication: Identity setup, JWT configuration, role/policy authorization - Integration: HTTP clients, message consumers/publishers, external API wrappers - Testing: unit tests, integration tests, test fixtures - DevOps: Dockerfile, CI/CD pipeline, environment configuration
artifact_overrides.test_case: - Test API endpoints with authenticated and unauthenticated requests - Test EF Core queries with in-memory database or test containers - Test business logic in isolation with mocked dependencies - Test integration points with stub/mock external services - Test concurrency scenarios for shared resources - Verify API response contracts match OpenAPI specification
artifact_overrides.architecture: - Solution structure: project layout, layer separation, dependency direction - API architecture: routing, middleware pipeline, versioning, error handling - Data architecture: EF Core context, entity configuration, migration strategy - Authentication architecture: Identity provider, token flow, authorization policies - Integration architecture: HTTP client factory, message broker, retry policies - Caching architecture: distributed vs in-memory, cache invalidation strategy - Deployment architecture: containerization, hosting, environment management
artifact_overrides.risk_entry: - .NET version migration complexity for long-running projects - EF Core performance issues with complex queries or large datasets - Dependency injection complexity in large solutions - NuGet package version conflicts and security vulnerabilities - Memory leaks from improper async/await or disposable handling - Breaking changes in third-party library updates
